selected_topic: National Fossil Day celebrated by baking sugar cookies shaped like fossils on October 13, 2026
news_angle: Commemorating National Fossil Day with fossil-shaped sugar cookies to honor history in a tasty way.
scene_metaphor: Hunk Mao dressed like an Indiana Jones-style explorer standing in a lively fossil dig site, baking sugar cookies shaped like dinosaurs, trilobites, and ammonites on a campfire stove. Around him, fossilized cookie crumbs litter the ground, and little cookie fossils peek out of the dirt. Nearby, cheeky tiny critters wearing paleontology gear excitedly examine cookies with magnifying glasses and brushes, while Hunk Mao juggles cookie cutters shaped like bones. In the background, a bakery stand with a sign reading 'Fossil Bakes' is run by another cat wearing a chef’s hat, and a humorous dinosaur skeleton made of cookie crumbs stands proudly. Hidden props include a tiny fossil-shaped cookie jar, dinosaur footprints made of sugar, an ancient scroll with cookie recipes, and a mischievous mouse stealing a cookie bone. The scene is vibrant, full of action and visual jokes celebrating the fossil cookie theme.
image_prompt: Square digital illustration of Hunk Mao the orange tabby cat, athletic and stylish, in an Indiana Jones-inspired explorer outfit, baking fossil-shaped sugar cookies on a campfire in a lively prehistoric dig site setting. Midground shows tiny critters with magnifying glasses studying cookie fossils in the dirt. Background features a cookie bakery stand labeled 'Fossil Bakes' with another cat baker, dinosaur skeleton made of cookie crumbs, and scattered fossil cookie crumbs. Scene packed with tiny visual easter eggs like fossil cookie jars, ancient recipe scrolls, dinosaur footprints made of sugar, and a mouse stealing a cookie cookie bone. Hunk Mao has a mischievous confident grin, juggling bone-shaped cookie cutters. REQ TEXT: "Fossil Bakes"

caption: October 13th is National Fossil Day, and the coolest way to celebrate is by baking sugar cookies shaped like fossils! Hunk Mao's got the fossil cookie game on lock—history’s never tasted this deliciously wild.
